contextNote: Swedish kommuner fund K-12 schools, eldercare, and social services that Canadian municipalities don't, and police is a national service. Even with normalization the scope is structurally different — and the official budget PDF isn't extractable from automated tooling.
dataStatusNote: Karlskrona figures normalized to a Canadian-comparable municipal scope: K-12 education (Kunskapsnämnden ~1.75 Bn SEK), preschool, eldercare (Äldrenämnden), and social services (Socialnämnden) excluded so percentages reflect only services Canadian municipalities also fund. Police is also excluded as it is a national service in Sweden (the only municipal public-safety service is fire/rescue / Räddningstjänsten). Totals and normalized base are approximate pending detailed line-item transcription from the official Swedish budget PDF.

normalization.note: Excluded so percentages reflect a Canadian-comparable municipal scope. The 'totalOperating' field shows the un-normalized total (~3.5 Bn SEK) for transparency; bucket shares are computed against 'totalOperatingNormalized' (~945M SEK). Police is national in Sweden, not municipal.

nativeDepartments.name: Note: police is a national service in Sweden — not in the municipal budget
Note: regional/county transit (Blekingetrafiken) is funded by the region, not the kommun
Funded by Karlskrona Vatten AB (municipal utility company), separate from the kommun's tax-supported budget
Kultur- och fritidsnämnden (parks, libraries, sports facilities, culture) (estimated)
Miljö- och samhällsbyggnadsnämnden (planning, building permits, environment) (estimated)
Kommunstyrelsen + Kommunledningsförvaltningen (governance, admin, HR, IT, finance ops) (estimated)
Finansförvaltning — räntor och amorteringar (interest and principal) (estimated)
